title: King's Edge deliverable benefit schema
description: Authoring schema for deliverable-level benefits and evidence of success. Benefits describe value, not products. Products and outputs should be defined in delivery timeline steps and referenced from benefits where useful.

stepId.description: ID of the delivery timeline step that produces or enables the thing being referenced.

outputId.description: Optional ID of a step output when the output has a stable authored ID.

outputTitle.description: Optional title of a step output when no stable output ID exists yet.

note.description: Optional explanation of how this step or output enables the benefit.

statement.description: Short statement of the value created when the deliverable is used. This should not be a product or task description.

beneficiary.description: Who benefits from the value being realised.

benefitType.description: Broad type of value, for example articulation, evidence, improvement, institutional learning or responsible-use.

realisationPeriod.description: When the value is expected to begin or mature.

successLooksLike.description: Outcome-oriented description of what would be true if the benefit were being realised.

evidenceOfSuccess.description: Qualitative, usage-based, judgement-based or quantitative evidence that would show the benefit is being realised. This should test value, not just activity completion.

enabledBy.description: Traceability references back to outputs or steps already defined in the delivery timeline. Do not define new products here.

linkedOutputs.description: Alias for enabledBy when authors find that language clearer.

examples.title: Programmes can tell a stronger graduate futures story
examples.statement: Programme teams have clearer evidence and language for explaining what their programme enables for students.
examples.beneficiary: Programme teams, departments, students, applicants, recruitment teams and faculties
examples.successLooksLike: Programme teams can make confident, nuanced and evidence-informed claims about what their programmes enable.
evidenceOfSuccess: Programme stories are used in programme review, recruitment, open days or student-facing contexts.
Claims are linked to evidence and agreed with relevant programme teams.

enabledBy.outputTitle: First working graduate futures evidence pack
